pr.title: nix: attempt to speed up builds
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Draft/open tooling PR attempting to reduce Nix build-time closure size by trimming unnecessary native/compiler/gui dependencies and changing the Nix build output. It also adds an unexplained `#define dingus` in `src/Runtime/platform.h`. A bot issue comment reports `No changes` for GALE01, but there are no human reviews or inline review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Main Nix derivation adjusted to use smaller/no-compiler inputs and emit `ninja progress` output instead of copying `main.dol`.
MWCC package install narrowed to selected GameCube compiler versions.
Objdiff Rust package build excludes `objdiff-gui` in addition to `objdiff-wasm`.
Runtime platform header receives an unexplained `#define dingus` addition.

what_changed.text: Replaced Nix `stdenv` with `stdenvNoCC` in `.nix/melee.nix`, matching the PR body rationale that the build does not need a native compiler because it uses the special GameCube toolchain.
Replaced `python3` with `python3Minimal` in `.nix/melee.nix`, with the PR body stating only `configure.py` needs to run.
Changed `.nix/melee.nix` install phase from creating `$out` and copying `build/GALE01/main.dol` to running `ninja progress > $out`.
Changed `.nix/mwcc.nix` install phase from copying the entire package to copying only `GC/{1.1p1,1.2.5,1.2.5n,1.3.2}` into `$out/GC`.
Changed `.nix/objdiff.nix` cargo flags to exclude `objdiff-gui` as well as `objdiff-wasm`.
Added `#define dingus` near the compiler/platform macro block in `src/Runtime/platform.h`; the loaded PR text does not explain this header change.
A bot issue comment reports `### Report for GALE01 (47dc59b - 3ac98c1) No changes`.

smart_moves.text: The PR targets closure-size drivers directly: no native compiler in the main derivation, minimal Python for configure-only usage, selected MWCC compiler versions, and excluding an unused GUI target from objdiff.
The PR body separates CLI sandbox needs from developer shell needs by excluding `objdiff-gui` from the Nix sandbox while noting a GUI can come from PATH in a shell.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
The only loaded comment is a bot report saying GALE01 has `No changes`; this supports that the slice contains an automated comparison report, not human reviewer intent.

follow_up_queries: PR 2716 nix build closure size stdenvNoCC python3Minimal
PR 2716 objdiff-gui excluded from Nix cargoBuildFlags
PR 2716 mwcc.nix selected GC compiler versions
Nix GALE01 No changes decomp-dev bot 47dc59b 3ac98c1

notes: The PR body clearly explains the Nix dependency trimming intent, and the diff supports the listed Nix changes.
The PR is open and draft in raw PR metadata; no merge or acceptance should be inferred.
There are no human reviews or inline comments in the loaded slice.
The `src/Runtime/platform.h` addition of `#define dingus` is not explained by the PR body and should not be generalized as a convention or accepted header change.
The bot `No changes` report is PR evidence, but the intake did not independently validate builds or objdiff.

source_update_candidates.title: Nix closure-size trimming pattern for CI-like CLI builds
source_update_candidates.text: For curator review only: PR #2716 shows a draft attempt to reduce Nix build-time closure size by using `stdenvNoCC`, `python3Minimal`, limiting packaged MWCC compiler versions to selected GC versions, and excluding `objdiff-gui` from the objdiff workspace build. Evidence is PR-local and should not be promoted to a global standard without confirming the final merged state and current Nix behavior.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential tooling/path knowledge, but PR is open/draft and has no loaded human review or merge evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate unexplained `#define dingus` in Runtime platform header
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2716 adds `#define dingus` to `src/Runtime/platform.h` near MWERKS/ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN compiler macros, but the PR body does not explain the symbol. Curator should verify whether this was accidental, temporary, or tied to a build workaround before recording any path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: Header change is concrete but semantically unsupported by loaded PR text.

rejection_notes: Do not extract a decompilation lesson from this PR; the slice is primarily Nix/tooling and does not modify decompiled C bodies.
Do not treat `#define dingus` as an accepted naming convention, platform macro, or build requirement without further evidence.
Do not treat the bot `No changes` report as independent validation performed during intake.
